2604.24506.title: MIMIC: A Generative Multimodal Foundation Model for Biomolecules
2604.24506.paper_summary: Biological function emerges from coupled constraints across sequence, structure, regulation, evolution, and cellular context, yet most foundation models in biology are trained within one modality or for a fixed forward task. We present MIMIC, a generative multimodal foundation model trained on our newly curated and aligned dataset, LORE, linking nucleic acid, protein, evolutionary, structural, regulatory, and semantic/contextual modalities within partially observed biomolecular states. MIMIC uses a split-track encoder-decoder architecture to condition on arbitrary subsets of observed modalities and reconstruct or generate missing components of molecular state across the genome, transcriptome, and proteome. Multimodal conditioning consistently improves MIMIC's sequence reconstruction relative to sequence-only inputs, while its learned representations enable state-of-the-art performance on RNA and protein downstream tasks. MIMIC achieves state-of-the-art splicing prediction, and its joint generative formulation enables isoform-aware inference that further improves performance. Beyond prediction, the same generative framework supports constrained design. For RNA, MIMIC identifies corrective edits in a clinically relevant HBB splice-disrupting mutation without reverting it by using evolutionary and structural signals. For proteins, jointly conditioning on shape and surface chemistry of PD-L1 and hACE2 binding sites produces diverse, high-confidence sequences with strong in silico support for target binding. Finally, MIMIC uses experimental context as semantic conditioning to model assay-dependent RNA chemical probing, rather than treating context as a fixed output. Together, these results position MIMIC's aligned multimodal generative modeling as a strong foundation for unifying representation learning, conditional prediction, and constrained biomolecular design within a single model.
2604.24506.paper_summary_zh: 生物功能源於序列、結構、調控、演化和細胞背景之間的耦合約束，但大多數生物學的基礎模型都是在單一模態或固定的前向任務中訓練的。我們提出了MIMIC，一個生成性多模態基礎模型，訓練於我們新近策劃和對齊的數據集LORE，將核酸、蛋白質、演化、結構、調控和語義/上下文模態連接於部分觀察的生物分子狀態中。MIMIC使用分軌編碼器-解碼器架構，根據觀察模態的任意子集進行條件化，並重建或生成基因組、轉錄組和蛋白質組中分子狀態的缺失組件。多模態條件化相對於僅使用序列的輸入，持續改善了MIMIC的序列重建，而其學習到的表示使其在RNA和蛋白質下游任務上達到最先進的性能。MIMIC實現了最先進的剪接預測，其聯合生成公式使得考慮同種異構體的推斷進一步提升了性能。超越預測，相同的生成框架支持約束設計。對於RNA，MIMIC在一個臨床相關的HBB剪接破壞突變中識別出修正編輯，而不需要將其還原，這是通過利用演化和結構信號實現的。對於蛋白質，對PD-L1和hACE2結合位點的形狀和表面化學進行聯合條件化，產生多樣的、高置信度的序列，並對目標結合提供強大的計算支持。最後，MIMIC將實驗上下文用作語義條件，以建模依賴於檢測的RNA化學探測，而不是將上下文視為固定輸出。綜合這些結果，MIMIC的對齊多模態生成建模被定位為統一表示學習、條件預測和約束生物分子設計的強大基礎。
2604.24506.author: Siavash Golkar et.al.
2604.24506.authors: Siavash Golkar, Jake Kovalic, Irina Espejo Morales, Samuel Sledzieski, Minhuan Li, Ksenia Sokolova, Geraud Krawezik, Alberto Bietti, Claudia Skok Gibbs, Roman Klypa, Shengwei Xiong, Francois Lanusse, Liam Parker, Kyunghyun Cho, Miles Cranmer, Tom Hehir, Michael McCabe, Lucas Meyer, Rudy Morel, Payel Mukhopadhyay, Mariel Pettee, Helen Qu, Jeff Shen, David Fouhey, Hadi Sotoudeh, Vikram Mulligan, Pilar Cossio, Sonya M.
